data.title: Rails M&A: Digging into Union Pacific, Norfolk Southern, CSX, and the BNSF wildcard
data.description:
The rails sector is on high alert following reports that Union Pacific (UNP) is focused on acquiring Norfolk Southern (NYSE:NSC), which could create the nation's first coast-to-coast rails network if the deal cleared regulatory and White House scrutiny.

Wells Fargo analyst Christian Wetherbee said the development is obviously a material positive to Norfolk Southern's (NYSE:NSC) share price as the negotiations play out. However, Wetherbee also pointed to the relative strategic fit of BNSF and Norfolk Southern (NYSE:NSC). The view is that the upside from that potential combination could compel the Berkshire Hathaway-owned (BRK.A) (BRK.B) company to make a competing offer for Norfolk Southern (NSC).

The firm also thinks investors should take a look at Union Pacific's (UNP) share price amid the flurry of M&A news. "We continue to think the path to M&A runs through the White House, UP's relative valuation has moderated amid the M&A news flow, while Norfolk and CSX have re-rated higher on the potential of a deal," highlighted Wetherbee.

A transcontinental rails deal is seen as creating compelling value for Union Pacific (UNP) and warranting a material re-rating in shares as the deal gets closer to completion, similar to how Canadian Pacific (CP) traded during the period before and after the acquisition of Kansas City Southern.

Deutsche Bank also weighed in positively on Union Pacific (UNP) on Friday, setting a price target that implies +20% upside.

Shares of Norfolk Southern (NYSE:NSC) rose 3.8% in premarket trading on Friday after gaining 3.7% on Thursday. CSX Corporation (CSX) added 2.0% in the early session after rising 3.7% on Thursday. Union Pacific (UNP) inched 0.4% higher premarket. Of the rails group, Norfolk Southern (NSC) has the highest Seeking Alpha Quant Score.

data.title: China's Pop Culture Group gains on plans to enter cryptocurrency market
data.description:
• Pop Culture Group (NASDAQ:CPOP) Friday announced its plan to enter the cryptocurrency sector, with specific initiatives planned to launch in July 2025.
  • The move aims to integrate innovative cryptocurrency payment solutions into the company's core business ecosystem.
  • Pop Culture Group (NASDAQ:CPOP) +55% premarket.

data.title: MEI Pharma soars after $100M litecoin treasury move, first public LTC holder on U.S. exchange
data.description:
MEI Pharma (NASDAQ:MEIP) shares soared Friday premarket as it unveiled a $100 million private placement to fund its new litecoin (LTC-USD) treasury strategy, marking a groundbreaking shift in corporate crypto adoption. 

With this move, MEI becomes the first and only publicly traded company on a national exchange to officially hold litecoin as a treasury reserve asset.

In connection with the closing of the transaction, MEI will appoint Charlie Lee to its Board of Directors and GSR as its digital asset and treasury management advisor to oversee the implementation of its litecoin treasury strategy.

Litecoin (LTC) is a peer-to-peer cryptocurrency that was created by Charlie Lee in October 2011. 

 MEI Pharma (NASDAQ:MEIP) +108.67% to $9.39.

data.title: Centene, Elevance among Obamacare insurers seeking sharp premium hikes next year: WSJ
data.description:
Centene (NYSE:CNC), Elevance Health (NYSE:ELV), and several other leading players in the Marketplace insurance exchanges established under the Affordable Care Act (Obamacare) are seeking sharp increases for their healthcare plans next year, The Wall Street Journal reported on Friday.

While Centene (NYSE:CNC) and Elevance Health (NYSE:ELV) are seeking double-digit rate increases in several states, Blue Cross & Blue Shield of Illinois and Texas, both owned by nonprofit Health Care Service Corporation, plan to hike their rates by 27% and 21%, respectively.

Meanwhile, the largest ACA plans in Washington state, Georgia, and Rhode Island are looking to hike their rates by more than 20% in 2026. 

The findings are based on an analysis conducted by the healthcare policy organization KFF at the request of The Journal, using publicized rate submissions of the largest ACA plans in 17 states and the District of Columbia.

The insurers have attributed the rate hikes to rising healthcare costs among enrollees and changes to government regulations, including a potential reduction to enhanced federal subsidies offered for ACA members at the end of the year.

The analysis comes amid a selloff of the listed players in the Marketplace business. Shares of Elevance Health (NYSE:ELV) fell ~12% on Thursday after the company, with its Q2 results, lowered its 2025 earnings outlook, citing, among other things, elevated cost trends among Obamacare enrollees.

Earlier this month, Centene (NYSE:CNC) pulled its 2025 earnings guidance, citing underperformance in its ACA plans business and other reasons. Molina Healthcare (MOH) followed up with its guidance cut several days later, noting elevated cost trends across all three of its business units, including its Marketplace division.

data.title: Porsche planning more cuts to offset weak sales, U.S. tariffs - report
data.description:
Porsche AG (OTCPK:POAHY) (OTCPK:POAHF) plans further cost-cutting as weak sales for its electric vehicles and impact from import tariffs into its largest market continue to plague the iconic carmaker.

According to a memo from the company’s CEO Oliver Blume viewed by Bloomberg, the company will begin negotiations with its labor union on further cuts later this year.

“Our business model, which served us well for many decades, no longer works in its current form,” Blume said in his memo to employees.

Earlier this month, Porsche (OTCPK:POAHY) (OTCPK:POAHF) warned that its second quarter bottom-line would take a hit of as much as €300M due to the impact from U.S. tariffs. During the first half of the year, North America was the only region showing sales growth for the company (+10%) as sales in China cratered 28%, and were down 8% in Europe, excluding Germany. In its home turf of Germany, Porsche (OTCPK:POAHY) (OTCPK:POAHF) recorded a 23% decline in sales.

The company’s path to improved profitability is hindered by several challenges, including a tough macroeconomic backdrop worsened by U.S. trade policy, supply chain disruptions affecting battery production for its EVs, and fierce competition in China—particularly from BYD’s (OTCPK:BYDDF) (OTCPK:BYDDY) Denza Z, which directly targets Porsche Macan and Mercedes-Benz (OTCPK:MBGAF) (OTCPK:MBGYY) buyers.

In 2024, the company reported a 390-basis point decline in return on sales year-over-year and 30% drop in after tax profits as manufacturing costs continue to climb.

Porsche (OTCPK:POAHY) (OTCPK:POAHF) shares are down 16% year-over-year underperforming German rivals Mercedes-Benz (-13%), BMW (-1%), and Volkswagen (-1%).

data.title: Trump Media announces Truth Social app for iPads
data.description:
Social media platform operator Trump Media and Technology Group (NASDAQ:DJT) announced on Friday the release of a Truth Social app customized for iPad devices.

Shares were +0.26% pre-market to $19.21.

The company said it will continue to stress test the app and update it based on user inputs.

Truth Social and Truth+ apps are currently available on iOS devices, Android devices, and on the Web.

data.title: Stock index futures gain after S&P 500, Nasdaq notch record closes
data.description:
Stock index futures ticked up on Friday, a day after the S&P 500 and Nasdaq notched record closes, helped by the retail sales report and earnings releases. 

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.1%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.43%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 2 basis points to 3.89%.

Building permits and housing starts rose more than expected in June.

Wall Street closed out trading higher on Thursday, with both the Nasdaq Composite and S&P 500 surging to fresh record highs.

“Markets have put in a decent performance over the last 24 hours, with the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq both reaching fresh all-time highs, with the global rally mostly continuing this morning. The advance was driven by another batch of positive U.S. data, including higher-than-expected retail sales and then a 5th consecutive weekly decline in initial jobless claims,” Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

With the stronger data and a continued rise in market inflation pricing, investors dialed down the amount of Fed rate cuts expected this year to 43bps, the lowest this has been since February. Reid added. 

“The problem for the Federal Reserve and markets is that the U.S. administration’s policy continues to create considerable uncertainty about the economic outlook,” UBS' Paul Donovan said. 

Sentiment is forecasted up slightly to 61.4 in the first reading for July from 60.7 in the June final.

“Political partisanship will be delayed in the Michigan consumer sentiment data (Republicans live in the best of all possible worlds, Democrats live in the worst of times—according to survey evidence),” Donovan said.
